reviews.review_text: I have spent all my fortune in steam games just for me to spend all my time playing the same game about a boy escaping his mom while crying, 10/10 recommended

As someone that despises and hates roguelikes with a deep passion, I have to say, TBOI Rebirth is pretty fun, especially with the characters and the items. The bosses and enemies are pretty fun to deal with as well....except for one particular abomination that I cannot forgive Edmund for: adding The Bloat. #RemoveTheBloat #IHateTheBloat

The roguelikes of all roguelikes, whether its just a casual run to blow off steam or just a game to sink a lot of hours into, this is it.
Isaac probably has over 1000 hours of gameplay/content and over 700 items to collect. The strongest point about the game is the amount of synergies one can get get. Compare this to say Balatro (which I also easily have 300+ hours on) which is also one of the greatest in this category but relies more on deckbuilding instead of the vast amount of items and synergies between these items like TBOI has. Truly no Isaac run will ever be the same. To finish this review, the most fun aspect I find is trying to find ways to break/mislead the game using the mechanics it uses to break you in the beginning. 10/10

I messed up and used the bible in my first fight with Satan, thinking it would one-shot him. Unfortunately it did the opposite and one-shot me. Needless to say I went to bed peeved off, but the next morning I got the dunk on ole Lucy first run.  10/10

usually fun game with unlimited replayability, highly recommend trying (make sure to play with all the dlcs)
🔪 🐶
Curse of the blind and Jacob + Esau are so ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ that I want to give up on video games altogether.

I know a lot of people worship this game, and I really wanted to enjoy it, but it just doesn’t click for me. Although I’m a fan of the roguelike genre and I find the overall atmosphere appealing, the movement and hitboxes feel off to me. It often feels like I’m skating all over the place without really having proper control over the character. The shooting also lacks impact, and I often can’t properly anticipate enemy projectiles.

The whole “you never know what you’re going to get” approach feels more discouraging than motivating. Other games use similar mechanics, but in those I still get a sense of progression and momentum.

I played for around five hours, but I can’t really say I enjoyed it. With other roguelikes, I usually get that “just one more run” feeling almost immediately, but that never happened here.

Sorry, Isaac — you little poop.
I never liked rogue-likes and I still don't a whole bunch but this game is groovy and took over my life entirely after purchase. I think you should play it

Just as fun and challenging if not MORE than the original flash version! Loved Binding of Isaac for over a decade now and highly recommend it to anyone looking for fun games with an interesting story!
game is great, but get ready to replace a couple monitors
I bought isaac in 2024, I just recently started playing again this year and I am so addicted. 10/10 game love u edmond.

This game really doesn't need an introduction. The Binding of Isaac is a certified classic, and I'm embarrassed to say it took me this long to finally play it. I enjoy roguelikes, but I was always put off by the 16-bit art style and edgy themes, so I never really gave the game a chance until now. I'm happy to say that I absolutely love TBOI and wholeheartedly recommend it to any roguelike fan.

TBOI is a hard game, and I definitely had a tough time during my first few hours. But once I learned the ropes and won a few runs, it started to click. The build expression and synergies in this game are absolutely insane and legitimately the best I've experienced in any roguelike. Every single run feels completely different, and there are hundreds of combos and items that can make or break a run.

I've barely scratched the surface, and I'm excited to dump hundreds of hours into this absolute gem as I continue learning it.
tboi is a great game,  vut takes a while to learn but thres a bunch you can do
One of the best games I've ever played. Hard to start with, but learning via trial by fire is the way it goes.

Bought the base game. I'm sure if you buy all the dlcs it gets better, but they really didn't care about maintaining the quality of the base game. Numerous softlocks, telefragging, and a slog to get through without the power creep of dlc items.

One of my favourite games, used to play it all day. Recently I got the urge  to play and  I find that its not supported for Linux and I cannot play.  How is this possible PLEASE add linux support, Proton is super glitchy and there is absolutely no reason for this game to not be natively on linux.

Hell yeah i recommend it, INSANE replayability, tons of secrets, i will say thought tainting every character is a bit tedious, i wish they all had their own way of being tainted. I like how mods dont interfere with achievements and gameplay either, it encourages modding and i love that. but PLEASE REMOVE THE BLOAT OR MEGA FATTY PLEASE OH PLEASE

Now that I’ve completed every achievement and clocked over a thousand hours on Steam and PlayStation, I feel fully qualified to leave a review.

The Binding of Isaac is, without a doubt, the best game I have ever played.

It has everything: difficulty, replayability, charm, depth, humor, amazing developers, and a vibrant community.

It’s endless fun—a simple game executed to perfection.

If you’re looking for a game that will stick with you for life, stop searching. This is it.
remove cross stone shooters from the game and ill lick your feet clean mr edmund mcmillen

This roguelike game is quite challenging compared to most. But, with the 34 characters that's in this game and the amount of enemies whether its normal, mini bosses, and bosses, You're bound to have very fun runs. This game also gives some laughs and references to the early 2000's. The devs did create such a nostalgic masterpiece aside from their controversies. I give it a 8/10 gamers!!!

This game is THE ROGUELIKE.

Grim, twisted and silly at the same time, The Binding Of Isaac is an action roguelike created by Edmund McMillen back in 2011, it's an unique, bizarre and fun title, highly symbolic, whose central theme explores both the good and bad side of religion and of fanatism itself. 

We descend into the depths, each stage harder than the former, each enemy scarier than the former, each boss stronger than the last one, our weapon? Our own tears, and the random items that we will find in treasure rooms, shops and chests, oh, but of course you could do deal with the devil to become stronger... Though if that is not your thing then maintain your purity being a good kid and eventually an angel room will spawn to gift you a blessing from the heavens, which you could use to kill your own mother, satan or manifestations of your own self, yup, pretty damn weird isn't it? 

With almost countless randomized rooms, enemies, items, bosses and power ups each run promises to be different than the last one, and there are so many secrets and achievements that will lock you into an addictive loop, though the gameplay itself is pretty addictive ngl

The game also features many unlockable characters, each one with their own stats and abilities, you can unlock secrets and achievements beating stages and bosses with them, most of these have their own unique play style, some of them are really fun to play with, for example Lilith and her mechanic of spawning minions and Apollyon and his mechanic turning passive items into stats boosts (These two are my favorite characters)

This game has so many things and is so fun to play that as many before me have said if you want to get the 100% you won't be playing less than 300 hours. I am not a fan of the roguelikes at all, but this game is definitely the exception to that rule.

10/10 - The best roguelike ever created.

The Binding of Isaac: Rebirth is a masterpiece wrapped in sheer, agonizing torture.On paper, the game offers infinite replayability with hundreds of items, unique synergies, and a diverse roster of characters. In reality, going for that 100% achievement completion (Dead God) is a brutal test of patience. 
Despite the massive item pool, the gameplay loop eventually hits a wall of crushing monotony. You find yourself doing the exact same chores, restarting runs for hours just to get a decent RNG, and grinding the same floors over and over again.It's a beautiful, addictive game, but the soul-crushing grind for achievements can turn an amazing experience into pure exhaustion. Play it for fun, but think twice before you commit to completing it
after 34 hours of this game and my friends comments as well, I can rightfully say that this game is the KING of rouge likes
I love the gameplay, characters, and the amount of stuff there is to do in this game. Getting rock bottom was a bit hard though.

The Binding of Isaac: Rebirth is one of the most replayable roguelikes ever made. Every run feels different thanks to the huge variety of items, synergies, characters, and challenges. The game constantly rewards experimentation, and discovering a broken item combination never gets old.

The amount of content is insane. Even after hundreds of hours, I still find new interactions, unlocks, and strategies. The difficulty can be brutal at times, but overcoming a tough run is incredibly satisfying.

The dark humor, unique art style, memorable soundtrack, and endless replay value make this a game that's easy to recommend. Whether you're a casual player or someone who enjoys mastering complex mechanics, there's always something to keep you hooked.

426 hours played. Still not done. Highly recommended. 👍

This game is certainly fun, but the multiplayer introduced is frankly more aggravating than it's worth. The desync issues and the lack of compensation or a patch for what could totally ruin a perfect synergy between two players are baffling. One could not describe the amount of pain I get when that awful "DESYNC" visual appears on my monitor. The worst part? It's somewhat predictable. It can happen randomly, yes, but there is also a specific set of items that when acquired cause the desync nearly 100% of the time. On the surface, it seems like such an easy fix! All in all, it feels like I've wasted countless hours on runs that end up ruined due to this single issue.

Personally speaking I have never tried my hand at rouge-likes until I begun playing Isaac, they're very enjoyable even if they can some times kick me when still down. A simple system with + great gameplay loop that's easy to start but obviously hard to master. I've been playing on switch so I have less hours on steam but I adore all of the fun and misery this game causes! Playing with more recent patches n updates feels great too, overall I adore this game its uniqueness is unmatched out of the park! Dead god percent i'm coming for you!!!

The Binding of Isaac: Rebirth is a roguelike action-adventure classic with a distinctive 16-bit art style and mechanics that have influenced the genre since its release in 2014. At first, the game can feel challenging, especially when you’re unfamiliar with enemy behaviors and attack patterns. However, the more you play, the more intuitive it becomes. Bosses are varied, often surprising you with unique movement or attack styles. With its endless item synergies and replayability, it’s definitely worth investing a few hours - and likely many more

After spending a little less than 700 hours on this game and reaching 100% completion, I can say without a doubt that this game is not worth $60 (base game + dlc). This game is terrible and was a complete waste of my time, for anyone planning on buying this game, do yourself a favor and skip it, your life will be consumed and you will never be the same after.

i love everything abt this game im just literally not good enough to beat it, ik everything that happens so trust me when i say ik how far away i am being only at 1 moms heart defeat,,,, amazing game if u have even an ounce of skill

This game is genuinely the best game I have ever touched in my life. I bought this game eons ago. However, when I picked it back up, and I listened to that glitter bomb of an intro, my screen started glowing. I could never think of a game as amazing is this game. The 'saac is so peak. When you think of a game at the top of the steam game food chain, I don't think of this game, because this game IS the food chain. This game does everything right. The graphics and pixely goodness that blesses my monitor when this game is displayed, is GOOD, that my monitor AND my pc get giddy at the mere thought of me clicking the big ol' play button on steam. When I was blessed by this game and got the most OP ipecac, c section, lachryphagy, crickets body, rock bottom, glitched crown run that made my game EXPLODE when I clicked an arrow key, that, THAT, is when I realised I love this game. Every single run that I play on this saint of a game, I feel like I'm being rubbed and pampered by the best game steam had to offer. When someone asks me "do you think The Binding Of Isaac Repentance+ Beta is worth the £50?" what do you think I say? Yes? Absolutely ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ not. I say that this game isn't ONLY worth the £50, I say that it is worth the 3000+ hours of your life to get platinum god. The mind of the creator of this game, has to be studied, no-one will ever again make a game THIS good. I would genuinely get strapped down and beat with a pumper-nickle for a year just to be blessed by this game for a single minute. Please steam, GAME OF THE CENTURY. Actually no. GAME OF STEAM this IS the game of steam, steam should rebrand to 'The Binding Of Isaac Repentance+ Beta distributer'. Please make the second game, there's no WAY it can get better that this

Soo I have a lot to say about this game i would liked if there was a meh review because this game enters in that category, game is fun, some characters are more good than others, some are utter garbage (lost, blue baby), some 80% of the items are garbage againts late game bosses like hush, delirium, mega satan, is just like reset until you get decent items, skill is an important factor but also having good stats too, tending to be a gameplay relient to RNG. If you enjoy torturing yourself for achivments go for it.
The OG roguelike is still endlessly fun, and the mod community is very strong.

This is my video game equivalent  to a comfort meal. Very challenging to be sure but also super rewarding with every new item, character, and secret you unlock. As you play you pick up little trick to get the most out of your run and sometimes the game almost makes you feel like you're getting away with something but in a good way. If you like roguelikes, to me this is an essential play

I used to have 600 hours and a death certificate on a pirated copy but I am GLAD to play the original this game really carried my last year or 2 I am glad I can buy it to support the game and the creators now

Personally, I think this is one of the best roguelikes out there, like top 3.
There is a ton of replayability, and if you get the DLCs it's even better, like 10 times better.

There are a lot of characters, strategies and synergies to find, along with many secrets to uncover.
The enemy variation is also nice, however you'll find some of them annoying or even some room layouts but I guess that's part of the charm. The story of the game is also a nice addition, makes the game feel complete.

And last but not least, this game has a ton of memorable bosses, most of them are crazy the first time you encounter them but it just gets easier and easier the better you get at the game. 
So it's not only satisfying to have knowledge about the items and mechanics but also see how you the player improve at the game with time and each retry.
This game is realy fun for the skill players. I played this game for 10 hours and i didn t beat it. The game is sometimes fun and sometimes hard i recommend this game for super good skill players.

Its Overall an amazing game. the only real con ive seen and had ingame is that its settings are pretty dissapointing in the base game without dlc's and that modding pretty much only exists for the game with dlc's active. The game's graphic is 10/10, the story is 10/10, Gameplay and mechanics 10/10, creativity 30/10, Overall 50/10

The Binding of Isaac Rebirth is a really fun roguelike game that I've played for years through every DLC, I love this game as many many others do aswell. As funnily grotesque as the game can be, it brings me a lot of comfort and consistency that you don't always get from games. Every run is fresh, with no single run being completely the same due to the thousands of room layouts and several floor types. There's so many choices and I'm never bored playing this game!

this game has the absolute worst aiming system for having tiny enemies, only being able to shoot in for directions is stupid when the mouse is right here. the screen often gets way to overcluttered and crowded so even having the high-visibility on for enemy bullets means nothing 90% of the time with an actual item set up you get hit by things you genuinely can't see because enemy attacks are on a lower layer than yours, enemy trails especially in the womb area are next to impossible to see because for some reason an entirely red background with a red screen affect needs enemies that leave a red trail of damage. still lots of creative potential within the game to correct every single issue I have if you find the exact right items every single run which will never happen with any amount of consistency.
